microsoft_ft: **Forward-deployed** Azure resilience operations across enterprise customers (Databricks, Walmart, SAP, NetApp), standardizing end-to-end validation workflows to sustain **45+ annual drills** and drive $14M+ in measured business impact.

Led 0→1 build of an internal Resilience Automation Platform, defining self-service scheduling requirements and edge-deployment workflows that cut operational toil by **30%** and transitioned execution to a scalable, distributed model.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level drill program — delivered in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate** — surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a continuous validation model across distributed infrastructure.

Served as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a **$1.5B+ enterprise contract**, directing 14 cross-org recovery executions under executive visibility across hybrid on-prem and cloud environments.
Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, reducing planning cycle time by 39% and expanding drill capacity by 21% without adding headcount.
microsoft_2023: Drove adoption of **AI-powered code generation** workflows across 14 Azure service teams through hands-on user demos and training, saving 37 engineering hours monthly and validating a new developer tooling paradigm.

Conducted **11+ structured user interviews** with Azure engineering teams to surface critical feature gaps, directly influencing the product roadmap to prioritize intent-based YAML generation for automated infrastructure pipelines.

Migrated documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** platform and enforced rigorous metadata standards, cutting engineer lookup time by 83% across distributed data pipelines serving hundreds of internal users.
microsoft_2022: Secured cross-functional alignment across **140+ teams** on a unified automation prioritization framework, accelerating region launches by 28% and generating $3M in pull-forward revenue.

Led discovery with 20+ service teams to map **81 hours of manual toil** per region launch, then built a roadmap to automate critical deployment paths — directly reducing edge-site operational burden at scale.
Engineered a **Power BI dashboard** to surface automation gaps and toil metrics across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to target high-impact investments in distributed deployment workflows.
amazon_robotics: Defined the **OS migration strategy** for a 2,000+ unit robotics fleet, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ edge stations and achieving zero operational downtime throughout the pilot transition.

Drove alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to deploy **automated CI/CD pipelines** for edge software, accelerating the deployment cycle by 25% across a distributed, on-prem robotics environment.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies and owned backlog prioritization, resolving **40+ high-priority tickets** to stabilize the developer intake process and unblock firmware deployment to field-deployed units.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by owning end-to-end scoping and invoicing workflows for 200+ monthly proposals, deploying a new CRM process that standardized the customer engagement lifecycle.
Cut Customer Acquisition Cost by **13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a data-driven go-to-market strategy, optimizing digital channels and aligning messaging to end-user mission needs.

Conducted profitability analysis across **1,000+ project records** to identify margin leaks, delivering pricing model improvements that directly informed leadership's operational investment decisions.

tailoring_notes: Wove 'forward-deployed' and 'edge deployment' directly into microsoft_ft bullet 1 and 2, mirroring JD's FDE identity and edge software requirements.
Surfaced 'hybrid on-prem and cloud environments' in microsoft_ft bullet 4 and 'distributed, on-prem' in amazon_robotics bullet 2, matching JD's hybrid operating environment preference.

Used 'end-to-end validation,' 'distributed infrastructure,' and 'mission needs' framing across microsoft_ft to mirror JD language around designing and delivering end-to-end capabilities for real missions.

Referenced 'edge stations,' 'firmware deployment to field-deployed units,' and 'edge-site operational burden' in amazon_robotics and microsoft_2022 to align with JD's DDIL/edge software and disconnected DevOps domains.
Framed microsoft_2023 user interviews as 'structured user interviews to surface mission requirements,' echoing JD's requirement to engage end-users and conduct valuable user testing.
